## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On April 21, 1526, the absolute, most massive and historically defining military engagement of the subcontinent occurred: the First Battle of Panipat. The massively outnumbered forces of Babur aggressively confronted the massive, unwieldy army of Ibrahim Lodi, the Sultan of Delhi. Utilizing incredibly advanced field artillery and massive, highly disciplined cavalry tactics, Babur achieved a massive, crushing victory. This monumental triumph absolutely annihilated the Delhi Sultanate and formally, aggressively established the massive foundation of the Mughal Empire in India.

On April 21, 1938, Muhammad Iqbal, a massive, brilliant philosopher, incredibly profound poet, and highly influential politician, passed away in Lahore. While widely revered for his massive, deeply spiritual Urdu and Persian poetry, he achieved absolute historical prominence as the massive ideological architect of the Pakistan Movement. His highly aggressive, massive 1930 presidential address to the All-India Muslim League heavily outlined the absolute, foundational vision for a separate, independent Muslim state in northwestern India, fundamentally directing the massive trajectory of the subcontinent's partition.
